_README: Real CIP-30 signData outputs from third-party wallets (Lace, Eternl, ...). Each entry is verified by test/cross_wallet_verify_test.dart against the native verifyData, proving our COSE_Sign1 + identity-binding implementation is spec-interoperable with shipping wallets. The 'signatures' array is empty by design — see docs/cross-wallet-verify.md for how to capture a real one and paste it here. While empty, the test SKIPS (CI stays green).

_schema.wallet: wallet name + version, e.g. 'lace 1.20' (for the record only)
_schema.message: the human-readable string that was signed (for the record only)
_schema.payloadHex: REQUIRED — hex of the exact payload passed to signData (what the wallet signed)
_schema.addressHex: OPTIONAL — hex of the signer address; if present, verification is pinned to it (identity binding is enforced regardless)
_schema.signature: REQUIRED — COSE_Sign1 hex returned by the wallet's signData
_schema.key: REQUIRED — COSE_Key hex returned by the wallet's signData
_schema.expectAccept: OPTIONAL bool (default true) — set false for a deliberately-bad fixture
